confirmatory_tests: Serum protein electrophoresis for monoclonal gammopathy
immediate_management: If PCV < 20% or clinical signs severe: emergency blood transfusion with cross‑match compatible feline packed RBCs
Start corticosteroid therapy: prednisolone 1 mg/kg PO q12h or methylprednisolone IV 2–4 mg/kg q24h
Consider antithymocyte globulin (ATG) if refractory after 48–72 h of steroids
IV fluid therapy with isotonic crystalloids to maintain hydration and renal perfusion
Monitor for adverse effects: GI ulceration, hyperglycemia
monitoring_plan: CBC with smear every 24–48 h until stable
Total bilirubin and liver enzymes daily until normal
Repeat Coombs test in 7 days to assess immune activity
Daily physical exam for signs of relapse or complications
Adjust steroid dose based on response and side effects
long_term_plan: Taper steroids over 4–6 weeks once stable, monitoring CBC and bilirubin weekly
Consider adding a second immunosuppressant (cyclophosphamide, azathioprine) if relapse occurs
Owner education on signs of anemia recurrence (pallor, lethargy, jaundice)
Annual re‑evaluation with CBC and Coombs test for early detection of relapse
